headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Cook Islands (rotation backfill, never assessed). Published composite 50.0 / Functional (rank 46) sustained against compound structural evidence: (1) free-association sovereign with New Zealand under 1965 Cook Islands Constitution Act — well-functioning Westminster-style parliamentary democracy; (2) Cook Islanders are New Zealand citizens with right of abode in NZ — distinctive equity arrangement that mitigates small-state vulnerability but also produces depopulation pressure; (3) Marae Moana marine protected area (~1.9 million sq km) — globally significant systemic-environmental positioning; (4) Pacific Islands Forum member, COP/climate-vulnerability leadership; (5) MAY 2026 Women's Parliament event (in-window context): leadership, equality, disability rights themed agenda — institutional empathic-regard signal; (6) ongoing depopulation challenge (~15,000 resident population vs. 80,000+ Cook Islanders in NZ/Australia) — structural equity-of-access concern; (7) standard Westminster accountability institutions (ombudsman, Audit Office). No in-window news events warranting movement. Dimensional profile (AWR 3, EMP 3, ACT 3, EQU 2.5, BND 3.5, ACC 3, SYS 3, INT 3) consistent with evidence. Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 50.0 / Functional; Medium confidence (structural baseline, limited in-window news).
key_evidence: Constitutional framework: 1965 Cook Islands Constitution Act establishes free-association sovereignty with New Zealand; Westminster-style parliamentary democracy.
Cook Islanders are New Zealand citizens with right of abode in NZ; distinctive equity arrangement.
Marae Moana marine protected area (~1.9 million sq km) — significant systemic-environmental positioning.
Pacific Islands Forum member; climate-vulnerability leadership; COP advocacy on Pacific climate adaptation finance.
May 2026 Women's Parliament event (in-window context): leadership, equality, disability rights themed — institutional empathic-regard signal.
Depopulation challenge: ~15,000 resident population vs. ~80,000+ Cook Islanders in NZ/Australia — structural equity-of-access concern; service-provision capacity affected by population dispersal.
Westminster accountability institutions present (ombudsman, Audit Office).
No in-window news events warranting movement.

evidence.finding: Constitutional framework, free-association status, Westminster democracy structure.
Marine protected area (~1.9M sq km); systemic-environmental policy positioning.
Climate-leadership engagement; regional coordination on Pacific resilience.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained. Standard Westminster suffering-detection institutional capacity (ombudsman, audit office); Pacific-state vulnerability-monitoring through PIF coordination.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained. Women's Parliament leadership/equality/disability-rights agenda indicates institutional empathic-regard structure.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 sustained. Universal health and education services functional; small-state service-delivery capacity limited by population dispersal.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained. NZ-citizenship arrangement is distinctive equity feature but produces internal disparities (resident vs. diaspora access); women's representation gaps; small-island accessibility limits.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.5 sustained. Free-association constitutional framework provides clear scope-clarity boundary; Marae Moana represents enacted systemic boundary on extractive activity.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained. Standard parliamentary accountability institutions; small-state limited investigative resource capacity.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained. Marae Moana = systemic-environmental policy structure; PIF climate-leadership coalition engagement.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained. Climate-vulnerability advocacy positions consistent with declared values; sustained engagement across leadership transitions.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 50.0. No math-hygiene flag. Sub-threshold delta. First agent baseline confirms Functional placement.

negativeSignals: Resident depopulation pressure; small-state service-delivery capacity constraints; women's parliamentary representation gaps; limited investigative resource capacity for accountability institutions; climate-vulnerability exposure.
positiveSignals: Marae Moana (~1.9M sq km marine protected area); Cook Islands citizenship right of abode in NZ; peaceful Westminster democracy; PIF climate-leadership; Women's Parliament agenda on equality/disability; constitutional stability.
sector_context: Pacific small island state / climate-vulnerability cluster. Cross-reference: Palau (May 8 baseline 53.1), Samoa (May 8 baseline 50.0), Niue (free-association NZ counterpart). Cook Islands distinguished by Marae Moana scale and NZ-citizenship arrangement.
open_actions_resolved: First agent baseline established for Cook Islands at 50.0 / Functional
open_actions_carried: Watch: any Marae Moana enforcement or scope changes
